title: Enable and use
html:
Enable and use

Add to My Topics

Feedback
  • Last UpdatedMärz 04, 2026
    • Uniface
    • 10.4

Uniface UX is provided as part of the Uniface installation and patch distribution.

The UX field widgets are defined in the [WEBWIDGETS] section, UX entity widgets are defined in the [WEBENTITYWIDGETS] section, and UX component widgets are defined in the [WEBCOMPONENTWIDGETS] section of the usys.ini file.

To enable and use Uniface UX, perform the following tasks:

Using UX Templates

The fastest way to create UX components is to use the inbuilt UX templates available in the Uniface IDE Resource Browser.

These templates are ready to use and include the appropriate widget configurations.

New UX Component Templates
  • Component: ux-Layout
  • Component: ux-HeaderFooter
  • Modeled Component: ux-Layout
  • Modeled Component: ux-HeaderFooter
New UX Entity Templates
  • UX Multi Occurrence Data: ux-Layout
  • UX Multi Occurrence Data: ux-DataGrid
New UX Field Templates
  • UX Input: ux-TextField, ux-TextArea, ux-PlainText, ux-UrlField, ux-PasswordField, ux-EmailField, ux-Button, ux-NumberField, ux-CheckBox, ux-Switch, ux-DatePicker, ux-Select, ux-ListBox, ux-RadioGroup

To access these templates, import the updated utemplates.xml file from: <uniface-installation>\uniface\misc\ into your Uniface IDE.

Using Manual Widget Selection

Alternatively, you can manually create components, entities, and fields and select UX widgets using the Property Inspector.

For UX Component Widgets
  • Create a component (Dynamic Server Page) by dragging it from the Resource Browser into the project.
  • Open the component and change its widget property to one of the UX component widgets using the Property Inspector.
For UX Entity Widgets
  • Create an entity using the non-modeled template from the Resource Browser, or load an entity from the Model.
  • Change the entity’s widget property to one of the UX entity widgets using the Property Inspector.
For UX Field Widgets
  • Create a field using any field template from the Resource Browser, or load a field from the Model.
  • Change the field’s widget property to one of the UX field widgets using the Property Inspector.

Once a UX widget is applied to a component, entity, or field, it behaves like any standard DSP widget.

Related Links
  • [webcomponentwidgets]

    The [webcomponentwidgets] section of the initialization file contains logical widget definitions for components used in Dynamic Server Page (DSP) components.

  • [webentitywidgets]

    The [webentitywidgets] section of the initialization file contains logical widget definitions for entities used in Dynamic Server Page (DSP) components.

  • [webwidgets]

    The [webwidgets] section of the initialization file contains logical widget definitions for fields used in Dynamic Server Page (DSP) components.

  • Widget Reference: Dynamic Server Pages

    Widgets in dynamic server pages (DSPs) determine the appearance and behavior of fields, labels, occurrences, and entities displayed in the client browser.

  • Physical Widgets

    The list of physical widgets in Uniface.

  • Logical Widgets

    The list of logical widgets in Uniface.
